content:
Another place where passwords or logon tokens can be cached is in a cookie. Try combing through Thunderbird's saved cookies and delete any that reference Hushmail.

They can be found (in Windows) at <Tools | Settings | Privacy & Security | Web Content | "Show Cookies" button>.

content:
I suggest installing over the old one, assuming both versions came from thunderbird.net. To be totally safe (assuming Windows), you could do this to backup the profile:


  1. ensure thunderbird is not running
  2. enter %appdata% in search window on task bar and press Enter key
  3. this should open windows file explorer in Appdata\roaming folder
  4. highlight the Thunderbird folder and copy to external media

content: Assuming you know the actual location of the 'extra' Local Folders, you can exit thunderbird and simplly copy the message file (ignore the msf entry) from one folder to the other. My apologies in advance, but I am absolutely new at this.

In one file I have: E/Resources/Data/TBirdData/Admin2/INBOX.sbd (which has 4 msf files in it). In another I have: E/TbirdData/Admin2/INBOX.sbd (which has four files with exactly the same names).

I want to move everything to the E/Resources file.

If I copy the contents of the sbd folder in E/TbirdData to E/Resources etc, will it simply overwrite the files with the same names, or append the data?

Obviously I want to add, not overwrite what is already there.

As you can no doubt see, I am baffled (and nervous!)
creator: dropmenu

content:
First, the msf files have NO messages and are useless. There should be a sister file without the msf suffix. Those files are the actual mbox file. And not the sbd entries. Second, if you use the copy feature of the OS, you overwrite, you do not merge. Third, if you want to merge, you need to do this:

  1. linstall the above-mentioned adon
  2. highlight Local Folders, rightclick and select the importexport option to import mbox and select the message file that you want to merge... give it a separate name.
  3. now, highlight the imported mbox, rightclick and select the importexport option to export EML files
  4. then, highlight the message folder to which you want to merge in the messages, rightclick and select the importexport option to import EML files
  5. you can then delete the mbox file you imported in step 2 above.
creator: davidsk

content:
I have encountered this as well - over the last 3 or 4 days.

Roger - what version / edition of Thunderbird are you using?

I'm using the Nightly Build - so I more or less expect these sort of things. Screen shots are below; My solution - has been to literally just wait until midnight then download the next days build - and I usually do fine for bit. But I have encountered this issue a few times - and wonder - what changed in Thunderbird's Cert verification setup. Openssl gives the imap.gmail.com cert a clean-bill-of-health ... so leaves me guessing.

If you click on the notification panel - you get the "Add Security Exception" dialog ... Which curiously - if you click the "Get Certificate" button - it doesn't get the cert - and "view" button greys out. I did try the - "Confirm Security Exception" - only to then experience the system saying that I have invalid credentials  :-(

Again - I'm fine handling the issue with daily build updates - but it's certainly a curious one.
